lineage: John of Damascus: Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
That the Deity is incomprehensible, and that we ought not to pry into and meddle with the things which have not been delivered to us by the holy Prophets, and Apostles, and Evangelists.

ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: That the Deity is incomprehensible, and that we ought not to pry into and meddle with the things which have not been delivered to us by the holy Prophets, and Apostles, and Evangelists.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: No one hath seen God at any time; the Only-begotten Son, which is in the bosom of the Father, He hath declared Him.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Deity, therefore, is ineffable and incomprehensible.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For no one knoweth the Father, save the Son, nor the Son, save the Father.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And the Holy Spirit, too, so knows the things of God as the spirit of the man knows the things that are in him.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Moreover, after the first and blessed nature no one, not of men only, but even of supramundane powers, and the Cherubim, I say, and Seraphim themselves, has ever known God, save he to whom He revealed Himself.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: God, however, did not leave us in absolute ignorance.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the knowledge of God’s existence has been implanted by Him in all by nature.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: This creation, too, and its maintenance, and its government, proclaim the majesty of the Divine nature.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Moreover, by the Law and the Prophets in former times and afterwards by His Only-begotten Son, our Lord and God and Saviour Jesus Christ, He disclosed to us the knowledge of Himself as that was possible for us.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: All things, therefore, that have been delivered to us by Law and Prophets and Apostles and Evangelists we receive, and know, and honour, seeking for nothing beyond these.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For God, being good, is the cause of all good, subject neither to envy nor to any passion.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For envy is far removed from the Divine nature, which is both passionless and only good.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: As knowing all things, therefore, and providing for what is profitable for each, He revealed that which it was to our profit to know; but what we were unable to bear He kept secret.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: With these things let us be satisfied, and let us abide by them, not removing everlasting boundaries, nor overpassing the divine tradition.
